KXCPI-26MAY-T0.4.reason: Tariff passthrough and sticky shelter costs support elevated monthly readings, but recent disinflation in goods and easing energy prices make 0.4%+ somewhat less likely than market implies.

KXCPI-26MAY-T0.5.reason: A 0.5%+ monthly gain requires above-trend readings across multiple components; tariff effects likely partially absorbed by May 2026, making this outcome moderately below the market price.

KXCPI-26MAY-T0.6.reason: Tail scenario requiring broad simultaneous acceleration in goods, shelter, and services; recent trend argues against this, roughly in line with Kalshi pricing.

KXCPI-26MAY-T0.7.reason: Very unlikely without a major energy price shock; slight upward adjustment from Kalshi given residual tariff uncertainty and sticky supercore services.

KXCPI-26MAY-T0.8.reason: Extreme outcome requiring simultaneous surges across all CPI components; consistent with Kalshi pricing as essentially a tail risk only.
